content:
Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>tool 的顯示訊息長度或內容是否還需要優化？</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-07-11</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
有差，而且差在三個層面。先講 bridge 的事實：`toolTimeline` 本身**完整記錄**整個 turn 的所有 tool call（`sessionManager.ts:692`，每 turn 重置），只是**顯示時**砍到最後 5 條（`sessionManager.ts:152` 的 `slice(-5)`，寫死的）——所以這是純顯示層的截斷，資料沒丟。

## 和 Claude app 的對照

| | Claude app | Bridge |
|---|---|---|
| 顯示數量 | **全部**，一個不漏 | 最後 5 條 |
| 位置 | **內嵌在文字流中**，出現在實際發生的位置（文字→tool→文字交錯） | 集中成一個區塊（現在在正文尾） |
| 細節 | 收合卡片，點開看 input/output 全文 | 一行摘要：emoji + 名稱 + 耗時，無細節 |
| 呈現時機 | 永久保留在對話記錄裡，可回捲 | 只存在於該則訊息內，下一 turn 歸零 |

## 為什麼 bridge 做不到（也不該做）Claude app 那套

1. **4096 字上限**：Telegram 單則訊息就這麼大。一個跑了 30 個 tool 的 turn，全列出來會把正文預算吃光——`renderReply` 裡 body 是扣掉 tools/thinking overhead 後才分配的（`telegram-ui.ts:434-438`），tool 行越多正文被截得越兇。
2. **沒有收合 UI**：Claude app 的「全部顯示」成立的前提是收合卡片——預設只佔一行，點開才展開。Telegram 沒這個原生能力（前面確認過 expandable 在 final 路徑做不到），所以「全列」等於「全部攤開」，噪音直接爆炸。
3. **內嵌位置做不到**：bridge 每幀是全量替換整則訊息，agent 的文字 buffer 和 tool 事件是兩條流，要交錯回正確位置需要記錄每個 tool 發生時的 buffer offset——做得到但複雜度不成比例，而且 5 條摘要的目的只是「活著、在做事」的進度感，不是審計記錄。

## 所以 5 條是對的取捨嗎

大致是。它的角色和 Claude app 的 tool 卡片不同：**Claude app 是完整審計記錄，bridge 是心跳指示器**。streaming 期間你要的是「它還在動、正在跑什麼」，最後 5 條足夠；真要完整記錄，資料在 `toolTimeline` 裡，可以做成 `/tools` 之類的指令按需查詢，而不是塞進每則訊息。

如果你覺得 5 太少（例如常跑長 turn、想看到多一點脈絡），把 `slice(-5)` 提成 config 環境變數是個 10 行內的小改動；但「全列」我會建議反對——理由如上第 1、2 點。

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content:
<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Read</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-07-11T02:09:02.399Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>G:\AI\telegram-kiro-bridge-main</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\sessionManager.ts\",\"offset\":640,\"limit\":110}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\sessionManager.ts\",\"content\":\"    }\\n  }\\n\\n  private applyUpdate(session: ChatSession, u: SessionUpdate) {\\n    this.resetInactivityTimer(session);\\n    const up = u.update;\\n    // Opportunistically capture usage-like meta the agent may attach.\\n    const meta = (up as { _meta?: Record<string, unknown> })._meta;\\n    const usage =\\n      (meta?.usage as Record<string, unknown> | undefined) ??\\n      ((up as { usage?: Record<string, unknown> }).usage);\\n    if (usage && typeof usage === \\\"object\\\") {\\n      session.usage = usage;\\n    }\\n    switch (up.sessionUpdate) {\\n      case \\\"agent_message_chunk\\\":\\n      case \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\": {\\n        const content = up.content as { type?: string; text?: string } | undefined;\\n        if (content?.type === \\\"text\\\" && typeof content.text === \\\"string\\\") {\\n          // Orphan turn: a message chunk arrived with no prompt turn in-flight\\n          // (typically a background sub-agent completion re-invoking the agent\\n          // via task-notification). The normal request→response flush path in\\n          // runPrompt isn't listening, so route it to the debounced orphan\\n          // flusher instead of the live-turn buffer. Thought chunks are never\\n          // user-facing, so only real message chunks take this path.\\n          if (up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_message_chunk\\\" && !session.inflight) {\\n            this.appendOrphan(session, content.text);\\n            break;\\n          }\\n          const wasEmpty = session.buffer.length === 0 && !session.thinkingBuffer;\\n          if (up.sessionUpdate === \\\"agent_thought_chunk\\\") {\\n            session.thinkingBuffer = (session.thinkingBuffer || \\\"\\\") + content.text;\\n          } else {\\n            session.buffer += content.text;\\n            session.charsOut += content.text.length;\\n          }\\n          if (wasEmpty) petState(\\\"streaming\\\");\\n          // Early-capture RELAY_DELEGATE tokens during streaming.\\n          // Workaround: Kiro CLI may strip <<...>> from final text in pure-text turns,\\n          // but streaming chunks arrive unfiltered. We capture complete tokens here\\n          // so the final-render path can fall back to them if normal extraction finds nothing.\\n          // Inline/fenced code examples must stay inert, matching final token extraction.\\n          const captured = captureRelayDelegateTokensFromText(session.buffer, session._streamingDelegates ?? []);\\n          session._streamingDelegates = captured.length ? captured : undefined;\\n          session.onUpdate?.(session);\\n        }\\n        break;\\n      }\\n      case \\\"tool_call\\\": {\\n        const title = (up.title as string) ?? \\\"tool\\\";\\n        const startId = up.toolCallId as string | undefined;\\n        if (startId) (session._toolTitles ??= new Map()).set(startId, title);\\n        session.toolTimeline.push({\\n          id: startId ?? `__auto_${session.toolTimeline.length}`,\\n          name: title,\\n          status: \\\"running\\\",\\n          startedAt: Date.now(),\\n        });\\n        // Tier A precedingTools 唯一 push 維護點（避免 tool_call+update 兩處重複計數）。\\n        session._recentToolNames = appendToolName(session._recentToolNames ?? [], title);\\n        session._toolInFlight = true;\\n        petState(\\\"tool\\\", title);\\n        session.onUpdate?.(session);\\n        break;\\n      }\\n      case \\\"tool_call_update\\\": {\\n        const status = up.status as string | undefined;\\n        const updId = up.toolCallId as string | undefined;\\n        // tool_call_update 只帶 opaque toolCallId、不帶 name。用 tool_call 起始時\\n        // 綁定的 title 解析工具名；退而用 inline title，再退用 \\\"tool\\\"。\\n        const toolName =\\n          (updId ? session._toolTitles?.get(updId) : undefined) ??\\n          (up.title as string) ??\\n          \\\"tool\\\";\\n        if (status === \\\"completed\\\") {\\n          markToolDone(session, updId, \\\"completed\\\");\\n          // A success clears any stale reflexion hint (fail streak is over).\\n          if (session.toolLog.length) session.toolLog = [];\\n          session._toolInFlight = false;\\n          session._consecutiveToolFails = 0;\\n          logEvent(\\\"tool_call\\\", { name: toolName, status: \\\"completed\\\" }, { chatId: session.chatId });\\n          // Tier A seq observation：僅 ACP 路徑（Direct 由 tool-loop 的 Tier B rich 接手，避免雙記 BC-1）。\\n          // slice(0,-1) 排除「當前工具自身」（tool_call 已先 push title），對齊 rich 的「preceding 不含本 tool」語意。\\n          if (session.activeProvider === \\\"acp\\\") {\\n            recordSeqObservation({\\n              name: toolName,\\n              status: \\\"completed\\\",\\n              sessionId: session.sessionId,\\n              chatId: session.chatId,\\n              precedingTools: (session._recentToolNames ?? []).slice(0, -1),\\n            });\\n          }\\n          if (updId) session._toolTitles?.delete(updId);\\n          session.onUpdate?.(session);\\n        } else if (status === \\\"failed\\\") {\\n          markToolDone(session, updId, \\\"failed\\\");\\n          session._toolInFlight = false;\\n          session._consecutiveToolFails++;\\n          logEvent(\\\"tool_call\\\", { name: toolName, status: \\\"failed\\\" }, { chatId: session.chatId });\\n          // Tier A seq observation：僅 ACP 路徑（Direct 失敗 tool 由 tool-loop catch 分支寫 rich）。\\n          if (session.activeProvider === \\\"acp\\\") {\\n            recordSeqObservation({\\n              name: toolName,\\n              status: \\\"failed\\\",\\n              sessionId: session.sessionId,\\n              chatId: session.chatId,\\n              precedingTools: (session._recentToolNames ?? []).slice(0, -1),\\n            });\\n          }\\n          if (updId) session._toolTitles?.delete(updId);\",\"numLines\":110,\"startLine\":640,\"totalLines\":1119}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

content.thinking:

This appears to be a question being asked in the primary Claude session, and I'm observing as a memory agent. However, I don't have any tool execution results or context about what work was actually done. I only have the user's question.

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
有差，而且差在三個層面。先講 bridge 的事實：`toolTimeline` 本身**完整記錄**整個 turn 的所有 tool call（`sessionManager.ts:692`，每 turn 重置），只是**顯示時**砍到最後 5 條（`sessionManager.ts:152` 的 `slice(-5)`，寫死的）——所以這是純顯示層的截斷，資料沒丟。

## 和 Claude app 的對照

| | Claude app | Bridge |
|---|---|---|
| 顯示數量 | **全部**，一個不漏 | 最後 5 條 |
| 位置 | **內嵌在文字流中**，出現在實際發生的位置（文字→tool→文字交錯） | 集中成一個區塊（現在在正文尾） |
| 細節 | 收合卡片，點開看 input/output 全文 | 一行摘要：emoji + 名稱 + 耗時，無細節 |
| 呈現時機 | 永久保留在對話記錄裡，可回捲 | 只存在於該則訊息內，下一 turn 歸零 |

## 為什麼 bridge 做不到（也不該做）Claude app 那套

1. **4096 字上限**：Telegram 單則訊息就這麼大。一個跑了 30 個 tool 的 turn，全列出來會把正文預算吃光——`renderReply` 裡 body 是扣掉 tools/thinking overhead 後才分配的（`telegram-ui.ts:434-438`），tool 行越多正文被截得越兇。
2. **沒有收合 UI**：Claude app 的「全部顯示」成立的前提是收合卡片——預設只佔一行，點開才展開。Telegram 沒這個原生能力（前面確認過 expandable 在 final 路徑做不到），所以「全列」等於「全部攤開」，噪音直接爆炸。
3. **內嵌位置做不到**：bridge 每幀是全量替換整則訊息，agent 的文字 buffer 和 tool 事件是兩條流，要交錯回正確位置需要記錄每個 tool 發生時的 buffer offset——做得到但複雜度不成比例，而且 5 條摘要的目的只是「活著、在做事」的進度感，不是審計記錄。

## 所以 5 條是對的取捨嗎

大致是。它的角色和 Claude app 的 tool 卡片不同：**Claude app 是完整審計記錄，bridge 是心跳指示器**。streaming 期間你要的是「它還在動、正在跑什麼」，最後 5 條足夠；真要完整記錄，資料在 `toolTimeline` 裡，可以做成 `/tools` 之類的指令按需查詢，而不是塞進每則訊息。

如果你覺得 5 太少（例如常跑長 turn、想看到多一點脈絡），把 `slice(-5)` 提成 config 環境變數是個 10 行內的小改動；但「全列」我會建議反對——理由如上第 1、2 點。

content.thinking:
The user is asking about whether the tool display message length or content needs optimization. Claude provided a detailed analysis comparing the Bridge's tool display with the Claude app, explaining:

1. The bridge records all tool calls in `toolTimeline` but only displays the last 5 (hardcoded in `sessionManager.ts:152`)
2. Differences between Claude app and Bridge:
   - Claude app shows all tools inline, Bridge shows last 5
   - Claude app has collapsible cards, Bridge has one-line summaries
   - Claude app keeps history, Bridge resets each turn
3. Why Bridge can't/shouldn't do what Claude app does:
   - 4096 character limit in Telegram
   - No collapsible UI in Telegram
   - Inline positioning would be complex
4. The 5-item limit is a deliberate trade-off - it's a "heartbeat indicator" not an audit log

The tool execution shows reading sessionManager.ts to verify the implementation details mentioned in the explanation.

This is a discovery/explanation session where the primary Claude is explaining how the tool display works and why it's designed that way. I should record this as a discovery observation about the tool display design and its constraints.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Tool timeline display design and Telegram constraints</title>
  <subtitle>Bridge shows last 5 tools as heartbeat indicator, not full audit log like Claude app</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>toolTimeline stores complete tool call history per turn in sessionManager.ts:692</fact>
    <fact>Display layer truncates to last 5 tools via slice(-5) in sessionManager.ts:152</fact>
    <fact>Claude app shows all tools inline with collapsible cards, Bridge shows condensed summary block</fact>
    <fact>Telegram 4096 character limit prevents showing all tools without sacrificing message body content</fact>
    <fact>Bridge lacks collapsible UI primitives available in Claude app</fact>
    <fact>Five-tool limit serves as streaming heartbeat indicator rather than complete audit record</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The bridge's tool display is fundamentally different from Claude app due to Telegram platform constraints. While toolTimeline records every tool call in a turn, only the last 5 are displayed to users. This is a deliberate design choice: Claude app can show all tools because it has collapsible card UI and no message length limits, allowing tools to be embedded inline at their actual execution points. Bridge faces a 4096-character Telegram limit where displaying 30 tools would consume the entire message budget, leaving no room for actual response content. The rendering logic in telegram-ui.ts:434-438 allocates body space after deducting tool overhead. The 5-tool window serves as a "heartbeat indicator" during streaming to show the agent is active and what it's currently doing, not as a complete audit log. Full tool history remains available in toolTimeline for potential future query commands.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>why-it-exists</concept>
    <concept>trade-off</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>src/sessionManager.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

<summary>
  <request>Analysis of tool display message length and whether it needs optimization</request>
  <investigated>Examined the tool timeline display implementation in sessionManager.ts, comparing Bridge's approach with Claude app's full tool display, and analyzing Telegram platform constraints</investigated>
  <learned>Bridge records all tool calls in toolTimeline but displays only last 5 due to Telegram's 4096-character limit and lack of collapsible UI. The 5-tool limit is intentional design: it serves as a streaming heartbeat indicator showing active work, not a complete audit log. Claude app can show all tools because it has collapsible cards and no message limits. Displaying all tools in Bridge would consume entire message budget in long turns, leaving no space for actual response content.</learned>
  <completed>Verified implementation details showing toolTimeline storage at line 692 and display truncation at line 152, confirmed rendering logic that allocates body space after tool overhead deduction</completed>
  <next_steps>No changes needed - the 5-tool limit is the correct trade-off for Telegram constraints. Could make it configurable via environment variable if user wants different limit, but showing all tools would be counterproductive.</next_steps>
  <notes>The analysis reveals a fundamental platform difference: Bridge must optimize for Telegram's constraints (character limits, no collapsible UI, no inline positioning) while maintaining streaming progress visibility. The design prioritizes showing recent activity over complete history, with full data preserved in toolTimeline for potential future query features.</notes>
</summary>

content.thinking:
The user is examining the tool display formatting code. They're looking at how the tool status emoji and tool names are formatted in the display. The grep shows:

1. TOOL_STATUS_EMOJI constant at line 113 with running (⚙️), completed (✅), failed (❌)
2. Line 157 shows the formatting: `${TOOL_STATUS_EMOJI[t.status]} ${t.name}${secs}`

This is showing how each tool is rendered in the display - emoji + name + elapsed time. The user seems to be investigating whether the tool name display needs truncation or optimization.

This is continuing the investigation from the previous question about tool display optimization. They're now looking at the actual formatting code to see if there's any truncation happening on tool names or if long tool names could cause display issues.

This is a discovery observation - the user is learning how tool display formatting works.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Tool display formatting uses emoji prefix and elapsed time suffix</title>
  <subtitle>Each tool rendered as emoji status + full name + optional elapsed seconds</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>TOOL_STATUS_EMOJI constant at line 113 defines running (⚙️), completed (✅), failed (❌) states</fact>
    <fact>Tool display format at line 157 is: emoji + space + tool name + elapsed time in seconds</fact>
    <fact>Elapsed time only shown for completed/failed tools when elapsedMs is defined</fact>
    <fact>Tool name is used verbatim with no truncation or length limits</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The tool display formatting implementation shows each tool as a status emoji followed by its full name and optional timing. The TOOL_STATUS_EMOJI constant maps three states to visual indicators: running (⚙️), completed (✅), and failed (❌). The rendering logic at line 157 concatenates these elements, showing elapsed time only for non-running tools that have timing data. Notably, tool names are displayed without any truncation - long tool names will appear in full, which could contribute to message length when combined with the 5-tool display limit. This full-name approach prioritizes clarity over brevity but may need optimization if extremely long tool names become common.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>src/sessionManager.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
